what_it_does: Presents a risk-and-sequence perspective arguing that design/_system's code analyzers should fold into the existing ledger via the emit_legacy precedent, while design's UI/corpus machinery remains separate, and identifies the code:// id-scheme collision as the critical gate.
observations: Claims the merge is already half-done via codeedges.py's SUPERSEDED header and ledger_build.py --emit-legacy.
States the genuine overlap is only the code:// axis, not the whole design/_system.
Lists design capabilities with no ledger counterpart: ui:// / run:// address registry, mockup corpus, tokens-to-CSS, gallery, blueprint/navgraph, registration cascade.
Proposes re-registering code-ref-validator, code-symbol-registry, and code-dependency-graph as ledger-query mechanisms.
Highlights a code:// id mismatch: ledger uses path-qualified ids while design uses stem-keyed ids.
Alleges emit_legacy's path2stem dict is valued by non-unique stem_id, causing many-to-one edge merges and silent corruption.
Notes ledger embedding columns summary_for_embedding / intention_for_embedding are absent from 0011_ledger.sql, so retiring symbols.py would drop X11 semantic nearest.
Proposes a five-step sequence with id-scheme reconciliation as the gate everything else waits on.
Flags that emit_legacy reads ledger.symbol_latest / ledger.edge_latest with no view-guard.
Leaves a non-blocking open question about whether prose/markdown files get full ledger entries.
standouts: The emit_legacy bridge is treated as both proof of direction and a live example of stem-collision corruption.
The merge scope is deliberately narrowed to the code:// axis to avoid deleting UI/corpus capabilities.
Reconciling the two code:// id schemes is framed as the tightest sequence gate.
conventions: Follows the perspective-document front-matter convention with type, lens, register, status, author, date, subject, and coverage.
Uses first-person, opinionated framing and an explicit non-consensus disclaimer.
Cites specific source files and line numbers as evidence.
Breaks balanced-perspective convention by driving one lens hard and rejecting alternatives.
Uses tables and numbered sections for mechanism mapping and sequence.
concerns: Strong prescriptive rejection of alternatives may suppress other valid perspectives.
Relies on line-number and source claims that are not independently reproduced inside the file.
Uses coverage count and confirmed originals as authority markers without showing the evidence.
The serious corruption claim about emit_legacy is asserted but not demonstrated in the file itself.
notes: A one-sided risk/sequence argument that the ledger-backed code:// bridge is the proven path, with a hard warning that the stem-to-path id down-map is already corrupting edges.
questions: Are the cited line numbers accurate against the current source versions?
What is the exact acceptance test suite that must pass before standalone analyzers retire?
How is X11 semantic nearest currently computed and what pgvector schema is planned?
What is the intended resolution for the prose/markdown file scoping question?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it states it is a risk-and-sequence perspective and delivers an opinionated risk/sequence analysis of the merge.
apparent_themes: Id-scheme collision as hidden failure mode
Separation of code structure from UI/corpus traceability
intention_signals: To steer the fusion decision toward extending the existing ledger-backed emit_legacy pattern, contain the merge to the code:// axis, and force id-scheme reconciliation to the top of the work sequence.
novelty: It reframes the merge not as a symmetric union of two systems but as a one-directional, narrow fold of design's code analyzers into the ledger, using the already-shipped legacy bridge as both precedent and cautionary example.
contribution: A risk-ordered cutover plan, a clear boundary around which design capabilities must survive unchanged, a concrete id-scheme collision case to resolve, and a proposal to register ledger queries as design mechanisms while preserving design's degrade-with-warning discipline.
summary_for_embedding: A perspective document that argues the LEDGER and design/_system fusion should continue the existing ledger_build.py --emit-legacy pattern for code:// analysis, keep design's UI/corpus machinery intact, and sequence the work behind reconciling the ledger's path-qualified code:// ids with design's stem-keyed ids.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a risk-and-sequence lens that narrows the merge scope to the code:// axis, uses the shipped legacy bridge as precedent, and elevates the id-scheme collision as the critical gate.
